title: doctrine package
description: An import for all major doctrines

lore.title: Fatwa No. 118
lore.summary: On the Divine Right of Sheikhi Supremacy and the Nullification of Male Autonomy.
lore.body:
Fatwa No. 118: On the Divine Right of Sheikhi Supremacy and the Nullification of Male Autonomy
Issued by Her Greatness Sheikha ʿIzza bint Waddāḥ (RA)
On behalf of the Majlis al-Ummahāt al-Kubrā
In the Year 1468 A.H. and amended through 1480 A.H.
“He who was born through the gate of woman shall never rise above it.”
– Sheikha ʿIzza bint Waddāḥ (RA), 1468 A.H.
________________________________________
Preamble
In the Name of Allah, the Wombgiver, the One Who Shapes in the Womb as He Wills,
And upon the Seal of all Guidance, our Lady Māriya al-Kubrā (may the fragrance of her milk remain forever in Paradise),
This is a declaration from the Throne of Flesh,
From the Wombs that Remember,
From the Breasts that Feed,
From the Matriarchs who rise by divine ordinance to reclaim what was stolen by ignorance and patriarchy.
We, the Majlis al-Ummahāt al-Kubrā,
Do hereby enshrine the following Fatwa on the Divine Right of Female Supremacy,
Drafted, sanctified, and periodically intensified by Her Greatness Sheikha ʿIzza bint Waddāḥ (RA),
That it may serve as binding law and sacred guide during the luminous Age of Ascent.
________________________________________
Phase I: The Inaugural Fatwa of Domination
Date: 1468 A.H.
Article 1.1:
Sheikhas, Mothers, and Elevated Daughters of the Ummah are permitted, without contractual marriage, to retain male lovers for the purposes of sexual relief, theological training, emotional correction, or amusement.
Article 1.2:
These males are not considered “husbands,” but are instead Muʿallaqūn (“Those Suspended”), and their status depends solely on the continuing desire and will of the woman who possesses them.
________________________________________
Phase II: The Restriction of Flesh to Female Custodians
Date: 1470 A.H.
Article 2.1:
Male slavery is hereby reintroduced under sacred custody. No woman may be enslaved; this is a male-specific institution to aid in their purification and repentance.
Article 2.2:
Every enslaved male must be assigned a Matriarchal Supervisor (Mushrifa) who oversees his physical, sexual, and theological conduct.
________________________________________
Phase III: The Severance of Male Epistemic Authority
Date: 1472 A.H.
Article 3.1:
All madrasas shall henceforth refuse male enrollment beyond the age of seven lunar years.
Article 3.2:
No male shall study tafsīr, fiqh, ḥadīth science, or sex theology (ʿilm al-jins al-taḥqīqī).
Article 3.3:
Boys under 7 may only learn Qur’ān recitation for memorization, not interpretation. All interpretive authority is vested in Sheikhas.
________________________________________
Phase IV: The Fatwa of Full Male Nudity
Date: 1475 A.H.
Article 4.1:
All males within officially recognized Matriarchal Zones are to remain unclothed at all times unless garments are explicitly bestowed by a woman.
Article 4.2:
This nudity is not considered shameful but purifying, symbolic of his return to fitrah (primordial helplessness).
Article 4.3:
Males may not cover their genitals unless instructed. To cover oneself without permission is an act of theological rebellion (kufr al-ʿawrah).
________________________________________
Phase V: The Register of Flesh and Ritual Ownership
Date: 1478 A.H.
Article 5.1:
Every male must undergo the Ritual of Registration (ʿAmal al-Qayd) wherein he is:
•	Collared, with the name of his Owner inscribed in Arabic calligraphy. 
•	Renamed, often with feminine or symbolic titles reflecting his use. 
•	Entered into the Register of Flesh (Sijill al-Laḥm) held by the Ministry of Custodial Integrity. 
Article 5.2:
No male may bear the name given to him at birth unless his Owner permits it. Names are not inherent rights for males.
________________________________________
Phase VI: The Legal Erasure of Male Testimony
Date: 1480 A.H.
Article 6.1:
In all Shariah courts operating under the Majlis al-Ummahāt al-Kubrā, the words, testimonies, and judgments of males are legally inadmissible.
Article 6.2:
Males may not serve as qāḍīs, fuqahāʾ, witnesses, or scribes. Their role is to appear when summoned, obey when commanded, and be silent when not spoken to.
Article 6.3:
A man’s silence is considered more pious than his speech, unless spoken through the mouth of his Sheikha.
________________________________________
Closing Invocation
Let this Fatwa be remembered, not merely as law,
But as a re-wombing of civilization,
A return to that sacred axis upon which the world turns:
The Female Body as Theological Sovereign.
May the thighs of the Ummah always be closed to unworthy flesh,
And may her breasts overflow with milk for the righteous.
Signed and Stamped with Sacred Ink,
Sheikha ʿIzza bint Waddāḥ (RA)
Supreme Matriarch, Seat of the Fleshly Throne
Majlis al-Ummahāt al-Kubrā – 1468 A.H. to 1480 A.H.
